headline: USC's ground game overwhelms Colorado in season-opening thriller
body: Miller Moss delivered when it mattered most, but USC's real story Saturday night was written on the ground. The Trojans' 161 rushing yards—nearly triple Colorado's paltry 56—imposed their will in the trenches and proved the difference in a 27-21 victory that felt closer than the scoreboard suggested. Both quarterbacks had productive afternoons: Shedeur Anderson completed 27 of 49 passes for 312 yards and three touchdowns, while Moss went 29 of 42 for 276 and matched Anderson's scoring output. Yet USC's defense, anchored by a relentless pass rush, kept Colorado's offense from ever finding rhythm in the second half.

The Buffaloes' inability to establish any running game became suffocating as the game progressed. Sweeney managed just 33 yards on four carries, forcing Anderson into a one-dimensional aerial assault that USC's secondary learned to time by the fourth quarter. Moss, meanwhile, benefited from the cushion his ground game provided. Marcus Myers chewed up yards behind an offensive line that controlled the line of scrimmage, creating those crucial third-and-manageable situations that allowed USC to control tempo and keep Colorado's offense sidelined.

Colorado's lone turnover—a fumble that USC capitalized on—came at a moment when the Buffaloes were trying to claw back into the game. That mistake, combined with USC's grinding methodical drive late in the third quarter, shifted momentum decisively. Anderson's arm talent kept Colorado competitive throughout, but arm talent alone doesn't win games when you're fighting uphill against a team that's dictating field position. This was USC's game to steal, and the Trojans did exactly that by doing the unglamorous work that doesn't always show up in highlight reels.
turningPoint: Late in the third quarter, USC's ground game produced a sustained drive that resulted in a touchdown, giving the Trojans a two-score lead that Colorado never truly threatened to overcome despite Anderson's passing production.

unsungHero.name: Marcus Myers
unsungHero.why: While Moss took home player of the game honors, Myers' 86 rushing yards on 12 carries established the physical dominance that allowed USC to control the game's tempo and keep Colorado's defense gassed. His consistent, punishing runs were the foundation that made Moss's job easier.
